title: contingency-dsl Operant AST (Universal)
description: Universal AST schema for the operant (three-term contingency) layer. Accepts ASTs from all compilation phases (1-3). For phase-specific validation, use: ast-parsed.schema.json (Phase 1: pre-expansion, includes IdentifierRef and RepeatModifier) or ast-resolved.schema.json (Phase 2/3: post-expansion, IdentifierRef and RepeatModifier removed). Execution engines SHOULD validate against ast-resolved.schema.json to reject unresolved ASTs.

program_annotations.description: Session-wide annotation defaults. Appear before param_decls in source. Schedule-level annotations override these for the attached expression. Optional field: absent ≡ empty array (unannotated program).

Annotation.description: A single annotation instance. Keyword is defined by the annotator that owns it.

positional.description: First positional argument, if any. Accepts any AnnotationValue (string/number/array/object).

params.description: Named parameters (key=value pairs). Time values (e.g., time=60min in source) are normalized to a number in the canonical unit (seconds by default) before storage; the original unit is not preserved in the AST. Values may be strings, numbers, arrays, or objects.

AnnotationValue.description: Value of an annotation argument (positional or params). Recursive definition supports string/number literals, arrays, and objects. Grammar: annotation_val ::= string_literal | time_value | number | annotation_array | annotation_object.

oneOf.description: annotation_object — map from ident to AnnotationValue. Keys follow the ident production (alphanumeric + underscore).

ParamDecl.description: Program-level parameter declaration. LH applies to all leaf schedules. COD/FRCO apply to all Conc combinators. BO applies to all Mult/Mix combinators. Expression-level overrides program-level.

TimeoutParams.description: Timeout parameters. Response-contingent removal of reinforcement availability (negative punishment; Leitenberg, 1965). Absorbed into the inner node's optional 'timeout' property, not emitted as a separate wrapper node.

resetOnResponse.description: Whether responses during TO restart the timer. Resetting TO is functionally equivalent to DRO (same contingency structure, opposite consequence polarity).

contingentResponse.description: 1-indexed operandum whose responses trigger TO. Required in multi-operandum contexts (e.g., inside Conc). Absent in single-operandum contexts.

ResponseCostParams.description: Response cost parameters. Response-contingent removal of a conditioned reinforcer (negative punishment; Weiner, 1962; Kazdin, 1972). Declarative structure only — token balance, bankruptcy, and state accumulation are recording-layer concerns, not expressed by the DSL. Absorbed into the inner node's optional 'responseCost' property, not emitted as a separate wrapper node.

amount.description: Magnitude of conditioned reinforcer removed per target response. Must be strictly positive.

unit.description: Label for the conditioned reinforcer being removed. Default: 'token' (Hackenberg, 2009). Common alternatives: 'point' (Weiner, 1962, 1963).

ScheduleExpr.description: Discriminated union of schedule expression types. LimitedHold is represented as an optional property (limitedHold, limitedHoldUnit) on leaf schedule nodes (Atomic, Special, DRModifier, SecondOrder, TrialBased), not as a wrapper node. Timeout is represented as an optional 'timeout' property (TimeoutParams object) on the same leaf nodes. ResponseCost is represented as an optional 'responseCost' property (ResponseCostParams object) on the same leaf nodes. Schedule-level annotations (grammar: annotated_schedule ::= schedule annotation*) are represented as an optional 'annotations' array on each schedule node — they override program-level annotation defaults for the attached expression. Three AST phases exist: (1) Pre-expansion: all branches valid, including IdentifierRef and RepeatModifier. (2) Post-expansion: IdentifierRef and RepeatModifier removed. (3) Resolved: post-expansion + LH default propagation applied (§1.6.1).

oneOf.$comment: Pre-expansion only. Absent in post-expansion and resolved ASTs.

Atomic.description: Parametric atomic schedule: dist × domain with a numeric value. Examples: FR5, VI60s, RT20ms

limitedHold.description: Temporal availability window duration. FI30s LH10s → limitedHold=10.

timeout.description: Timeout parameters. VI60s TO(duration=30s, reset_on_response=true) → timeout={duration:30, durationUnit:'s', resetOnResponse:true}.

responseCost.description: Response cost parameters. VI60s ResponseCost(amount=1) → responseCost={amount:1}. Optional unit defaults to 'token'.

annotations.description: Schedule-level annotations (grammar: annotated_schedule ::= schedule annotation*). Overrides program-level annotation defaults for this expression.

Special.description: Boundary schedules with no parameters: EXT (zero element) or CRF (identity = FR1).

Compound.description: Compound schedule: combinator applied to 2+ sub-schedules, with optional keyword parameters. COD and FRCO for Conc, BO for Mult/Mix.

annotations.description: Schedule-level annotations attached to the compound as a whole.

CombinatorParams.description: Keyword arguments for compound schedule combinators. COD/FRCO: Conc-only. BO: Mult/Mix-only. count/onset: Interpolate-only. target: Overlay-only. Semantic pass validates per-combinator validity.

COD.description: Changeover Delay: minimum time after operandum switch before reinforcement is available (Catania, 1966). Conc-only. Symmetric (scalar 'value') or directional ('base' + 'directional' overrides; Pliskoff, 1971; Williams & Bell, 1999). Discriminated on 'value' key (symmetric) vs 'directional' key (asymmetric).

oneOf.description: Symmetric COD: same delay regardless of switch direction.

Directional COD: per-direction delays (Pliskoff, 1971). Optional 'base' provides default for unspecified directions; 'directional' entries override specific (from, to) pairs. dir_ref identifiers are resolved to 1-indexed integers in the AST.

base.description: Base COD for all unspecified directions (from scalar COD=Xs).

directional.description: Direction-specific COD overrides. Each entry specifies a (from, to) component pair and the COD value for that transition.

FRCO.description: Fixed-Ratio Changeover: number of responses required on new operandum before reinforcement is available (Hunter & Davison, 1985; Pliskoff & Fetterman, 1981). Conc-only, optional.

BO.description: Blackout: response-independent dark period between component transitions (Reynolds, 1961). Mult/Mix-only, optional. Omission = no blackout (immediate transition).

count.description: Number of reinforcements during the interpolated block (Ferster & Skinner, 1957). Interpolate-only, required. Must be a positive integer; no time unit.

onset.description: Time on background schedule before interpolation begins (Ferster & Skinner, 1957). Interpolate-only, optional but recommended (linter warns on omission). Must be positive; time unit required.

target.description: Response-class targeting for Overlay combinator (Todorov, 1971). Overlay-only. 'changeover' = punishment contingent on changeover responses only (requires Conc baseline). 'all' = all responses (default; omitted from AST when implicit). Explicit 'all' and omission produce identical AST.

PUNISH.description: Response-class-specific punishment directive. Conc-only. Supports three target modes: 'changeover' (all changeover transitions), directional entries (specific from→to transitions; Todorov, 1971), and component-targeted entries (all responses on a component; de Villiers, 1980). Directional and component entries may coexist; 'changeover' mode excludes directional entries (constraint §88).

changeover.description: Punishment schedule applied to all changeover responses. Mutually exclusive with 'directional'. Shape follows ScheduleExpr.

directional.description: Directional punishment entries: punishment schedule per (from, to) component pair (Todorov, 1971). dir_ref identifiers resolved to 1-indexed integers.

component.description: Component-targeted punishment: punishment schedule applied to all responses on the specified component (de Villiers, 1980). Independent of direction.

Modifier.description: Modifier schedule. Discriminated union on the 'modifier' field (Issue #35). Each branch constrains fields to those semantically valid for that modifier subtype.

DRModifier.description: Differential Reinforcement modifier (DRL/DRH/DRO). Standalone usage implies an implicit CRF base schedule (i.e., DRL5s ≡ Tand(CRF, DRL5s)); when combined with an explicit base schedule via Tand or Conj, the explicit base replaces the implicit CRF. See defaults.md §Standalone DR modifier.

limitedHold.description: Temporal availability window duration. DRL3s LH8s → limitedHold=8 (Kramer & Rilling, 1970).

PRModifier.description: Progressive Ratio modifier. Step function (required) determines ratio progression across reinforcements. Bare PR without explicit step function is a parse error.

RepeatModifier.description: Repeat(n, S) — syntactic sugar for Tand(S, ..., S) [n copies]. Pre-expansion only: desugared to Compound(Tand) during semantic analysis. Absent in post-expansion and resolved ASTs.

annotations.description: Schedule-level annotations (pre-expansion). Transferred to the expanded Tand node during desugaring.

LagModifier.description: Lag schedule (Page & Neuringer, 1985). Reinforces response sequences that differ from the preceding n sequences.

length.description: Sequence length (Page & Neuringer, 1985). 1 = individual response (applied research default). N = sequence of N responses.

PctlModifier.description: Percentile schedule (Operant.Stateful: Platt, 1973; Galbicka, 1994). Reinforces responses whose measured dimension falls at or beyond a percentile threshold of recent responses.

pctl_window.description: Number of recent responses for distribution calculation. Default: 20.

pctl_dir.description: Reinforcement direction. 'below': at or below threshold. 'above': at or above. Default: 'below'.

SecondOrder.description: Second-order schedule (Kelleher & Fry, 1962). FR5(FI30) = 5 completions of FI30 produce reinforcement. Overall must be Atomic (no EXT/CRF). Unit must be simple (atomic) schedule (compound inner rejected as INVALID_SECOND_ORDER_UNIT). The overall schedule operates on unit completions, not individual responses: its value field counts the number of times the unit schedule must be completed.

overall.description: The outer schedule that arranges reinforcement contingent on unit completions. Its value field is always interpreted as a unit completion count, not an individual response count (see spec/en/operant/theory.md §2.11).

unit.description: The inner schedule that defines the derived response unit. Each completion of this schedule counts as one unit for the overall schedule.

limitedHold.description: Temporal availability window duration for second-order schedule completion.

responseCost.description: Response cost parameters. Applied to the second-order schedule as a whole: each target response during either the overall or the unit component triggers cost.

AversiveSchedule.description: Aversive schedule primitive. Includes Sidman free-operant avoidance (Sidman, 1953) and discriminated avoidance (Solomon & Wynne, 1953). The contingency structure differs from the F/V/R × R/I/T reinforcement matrix and requires a dedicated node type.

responseCost.description: Response cost parameters. Permitted on aversive schedules; empirical validity of shock + point-loss combinations is a research question, not a syntactic constraint.
Response cost parameters. Permitted on aversive schedules.

SidmanParams.description: Parameters for Sidman free-operant avoidance (Sidman, 1953). Both SSI and RSI are mandatory; no defaults. Formally: next_shock(t) = max(last_shock + SSI, last_response + RSI).

SSI.description: Shock-Shock Interval: baseline time between shocks in absence of response. Must be positive; time unit required.

RSI.description: Response-Shock Interval: postponement time triggered by each response. Must be positive; time unit required. Linter warns if RSI > SSI.

DiscrimAvParams.description: Parameters for discriminated avoidance (Solomon & Wynne, 1953). CSUSInterval, ITI, and mode are mandatory. ShockDuration is required for mode=fixed. MaxShock is optional for mode=escape.

CSUSInterval.description: CS-US Interval: time from CS onset to US onset. Must be positive; time unit required.

ITI.description: Inter-Trial Interval: CS-onset to next CS-onset. Must be positive and > CSUSInterval; time unit required.

mode.description: US delivery mode. 'fixed': US presented for a fixed ShockDuration. 'escape': US terminated by response (optional MaxShock safety cutoff).

ShockDuration.description: Duration of the fixed US presentation. Required when mode=fixed; forbidden when mode=escape.

MaxShock.description: Safety cutoff for escape-terminated US (Solomon & Wynne, 1953 used 2min). Optional when mode=escape; forbidden when mode=fixed. Null/absent = no cutoff.

IdentifierRef.description: Reference to a let-bound name. Present only in pre-expansion AST; removed after semantic analysis.

TimeUnit.description: Declared time unit. null = dimensionless (ratio schedules).
